action.reasoning: Observation: predict.py crashes because local test used /data which has no test.txt. The actual eval framework passes the correct test path, but predict.py needs to handle edge cases better. Also the CLIP model (val 0.7955) hasn't been evaluated on test yet. Best test score is 0.6174 from iter3 DINOv2. ~22 min left. Analysis: CLIP with internet-scale pretraining may generalize better to L100. Must fix predict.py to be robust, then submit eval on clip_best.pt. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with better error handling, submit eval on CLIP checkpoint immediately.
action.command: python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints/clip_best.pt', map_location='cpu', weights_only=False); print('Keys:', list(ckpt.keys())); print('Has ema:', 'ema_state_dict' in ckpt); sd=ckpt.get('model_state_dict',{}); print('Model keys sample:', list(sd.keys())[:5])"
